name_en: Society of Seminary Teachers of Qom
description: The Society of Seminary Teachers of Qom (Jame'eh-ye Modarresin-e Howzeh-ye Elmiyyeh-ye Qom) is an influential association of Shia clerics based in the Qom seminary. Founded in 1961, it became a key vehicle for [Ruhollah Khomeini](/people/ruhollah-khomeini)'s political movement and played a central role in the [Iranian Revolution](/events/iranian-revolution-1979). The society's members dominated the [Assembly of Experts for the Constitution](/orgs/assembly-of-experts-for-constitution) in 1979 and have maintained significant influence over the [Guardian Council](/orgs/guardian-council) and the [Assembly of Experts](/orgs/assembly-of-experts). It is considered a conservative-to-hardline clerical body closely aligned with the ruling establishment.
editorial_notes: Politically active since the 1960s. Not to be confused with the Society of Qom Seminary Scholars (Majma-ye Modarresin), a separate group.
